title: cascabela peruviana
intro: Cascabela peruviana, also known as the Yellow Oleander, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to South America. The plant contains various alkaloids, particularly cascabela alkaloids and secologanin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Cascabela peruviana has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The plant can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits and medicinal properties.
